LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT INVENSYS SYSTEMS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TIMELY. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. THE COURT DENIES THE MOTION BECAUSE: (1) MOST OF THE SPECIAL DISCOVERY THAT DEFENDANT CITES IS UNVERIFIED AND AS UNVERIFIED RESPONSE IS TANTAMOUNT TO NOTHING UNDER APPLETON VS. SUPERIOR COURT (1988) 206 CAL.APP. 3RD 632, 636; (2) THE DISCOVERY THAT IS VERIFIED DOES NOT SHOW THAT PLAINTIFFS CANNOT ESTABLISH THEIR CASE; (3) THE HAGBERG DEPOSITION IS INADMISSIBLE UNDER SPRAGUE VS. EQUIFAX, INC. (1985) 166 CAL.APP. 3RD 1012, 1042; (4) EVEN IF THE COURT ADMITTED THE HAGBERG DEPOSITION, HE STATED THAT HE SAW DECEDENT WORKING WITH DEFENDANT'S RECORDERS; (5) HAGBERG'S ADMISSION THAT HE DID NOT KNOW WHETHER DECEDENT'S WORK CAUSED ASBESTOS EXPOSURE IS NOT PROBATIVE BECAUSE THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT HE HAS THE REQUISITE FOUNDATION REGARDING THAT ISSUE; (6) DEFENDANT'S CITATION TO THE HEIRS' DEPOSITION TESTIMONY IS NOT PROBATIVE BECAUSE THERE IS NO SHOWING THAT THEY POSSESS PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE ABOUT DECEDENTS WORK ENVIRONMENT; AND (7) DEFENDANT'S SEPARATE STATEMENT DOES NOT COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1350(H) BECAUSE DEFENDANT GENERALLY CITES EVIDENCE. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT INVENSYS SYSTEMS, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion timely. Motion for summary judgment/summary adjudication denied, failure to shift burden of proof. The Court denies the motion because: (1) Most of the special discovery that defendant cites is unverified and as unverified response is tantamount to nothing under Appleton vs. Superior Court (1988) 206 Cal.App. 3rd 632, 636; (2) The discovery that is verified does not show that plaintiffs cannot establish their case; (3) The Hagberg deposition is inadmissible under Sprague vs. Equifax, Inc. (1985) 166 Cal.App. 3rd 1012, 1042; (4) Even if the court admitted the Hagberg deposition, he stated that he saw decedent working with defendant's recorders; (5) Hagberg's admission that he did not know whether decedent's work caused asbestos exposure is not probative because there is no showing that he has the requisite foundation regarding that issue; (6) Defendant's citation to the heirs' deposition testimony is not probative because there is no showing that they possess personal knowledge about decedents work environment; and (7) Defendant's separate statement does not comply with CRC 3.1350(h) because defendant generally cites evidence. Request for sanctions denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-08-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROCKWELL AUTOMATION, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion timely, court order did not bar this motion. Motion denied, failure to shift burden of proof. See Weber vs. John Crane (2006) 143 Cal.App. 4th 1433; Gaggero vs. Yura (2003) 108 Cal.App. 4th 884, 893. Request for sanctions denied. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
